questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a rhombus bisect each other at right angles and also bisect its angles.
questions.answer: Let ABCD be a rhombus with diagonals AC and BD intersecting at O. Using triangle congruence, ∆AOB ≅ ∆COD, ∆AOD ≅ ∆BOC, we get AO = CO, BO = DO, and diagonals perpendicular, also bisect angles at vertices.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a square are equal, perpendicular, and bisect the angles.
questions.answer: Since square is both a rhombus and rectangle, combine properties: diagonals equal (rectangle), perpendicular and bisect angles (rhombus).

questions.question: Prove that in a parallelogram, the sum of the squares of the sides equals the sum of the squares of the diagonals.
questions.answer: Let sides be AB = CD = a and AD = BC = b, diagonals AC = p and BD = q. Using law of cosines in triangles formed by diagonals, p² + q² = 2(a² + b²).

questions.question: Prove that the midpoints of the sides of any quadrilateral form a parallelogram.
questions.answer: Join midpoints of quadrilateral ABCD, use midpoint theorem: opposite sides of new quadrilateral are parallel, forming a parallelogram.

questions.question: Prove that a quadrilateral is a parallelogram if its diagonals bisect each other.
questions.answer: Let ABCD have diagonals intersect at O. If AO = CO and BO = DO, use triangle congruence to show opposite sides equal and parallel; hence ABCD is a parallelogram.

questions.question: Prove that a parallelogram is a rectangle if its diagonals are equal.
questions.answer: Let ABCD be parallelogram with AC = BD. Using congruence of triangles formed by diagonals, show that each angle = 90°, hence rectangle.

questions.question: Prove that a parallelogram is a rhombus if its diagonals are perpendicular.
questions.answer: Let ABCD be parallelogram with diagonals AC ⟂ BD. Use congruent triangles formed by diagonals to show all sides equal; hence rhombus.

questions.question: Prove that a square is a rectangle with all sides equal.
questions.answer: Square has all angles 90° (rectangle) and all sides equal (rhombus); hence square = rectangle + equal sides.

questions.question: Prove that a square is a rhombus with right angles.
questions.answer: Square has all sides equal (rhombus) and all angles 90°, satisfying rhombus with right angles.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a kite are perpendicular.
questions.answer: Draw kite ABCD. Using congruent triangles formed by diagonals, show diagonals intersect at 90°.

questions.question: Prove that a quadrilateral is a parallelogram if one pair of opposite sides is equal and parallel.
questions.answer: Let AB || CD and AB = CD. Use triangle congruence to prove opposite sides parallel, hence quadrilateral is a parallelogram.

questions.question: Prove that a quadrilateral is a parallelogram if its opposite angles are equal.
questions.answer: Let ∠A = ∠C and ∠B = ∠D. Draw diagonals and use congruence of triangles to prove opposite sides parallel, hence parallelogram.

questions.question: Prove that the sum of interior angles of a quadrilateral is 360° using triangles.
questions.answer: Divide quadrilateral into two triangles using a diagonal. Each triangle sum = 180°, total = 360°.

questions.question: Prove that a trapezium's diagonals are generally not equal and do not bisect each other.
questions.answer: Construct trapezium ABCD with AB || CD. Show by measurement or coordinate method that AC ≠ BD and diagonals do not bisect each other.

questions.question: Prove that the sum of angles on the same side of a transversal in a parallelogram is 180°.
questions.answer: Using AB || CD, AD || BC, apply co-interior angle theorem to show ∠A + ∠D = 180° and ∠B + ∠C = 180°.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a rhombus bisect opposite angles.
questions.answer: Let diagonals AC and BD intersect at O. Use triangle congruence to show each diagonal divides vertex angles into two equal parts.

questions.question: Prove that the diagonals of a rectangle bisect each other.
questions.answer: Diagonals AC and BD intersect at O. Use congruence of triangles formed by diagonals to show AO = CO and BO = DO.

questions.question: Prove that in a parallelogram, each diagonal divides it into two congruent triangles.
questions.answer: Triangles formed by a diagonal share a common base and height. Using congruence criteria, triangles are congruent.

questions.question: Prove that opposite sides of a parallelogram are equal using triangles.
questions.answer: Use diagonals to form triangles; by congruence of triangles, opposite sides are shown equal.

questions.question: Prove that a kite has one axis of symmetry along a diagonal.
questions.answer: Draw kite ABCD; show that diagonal joining unequal angles bisects kite into congruent triangles, forming axis of symmetry.

questions.question: Prove that the sum of squares of sides of a parallelogram equals the sum of squares of its diagonals.
questions.answer: Using vectors or parallelogram law, if sides are a and b, diagonals p and q, then p² + q² = 2(a² + b²).

questions.question: Prove that a rectangle is a parallelogram with equal diagonals.
questions.answer: Rectangle has right angles and opposite sides equal. Use Pythagoras theorem to show diagonals equal.

questions.question: Prove that a rhombus is a parallelogram with perpendicular diagonals.
questions.answer: Rhombus has all sides equal and opposite sides parallel. Diagonals intersect at right angles using triangle congruence.

questions.question: Prove that a square is both a rectangle and a rhombus.
questions.answer: Square has all sides equal and angles 90°; hence satisfies properties of rectangle and rhombus.

questions.question: Prove that the midpoints of sides of any quadrilateral form a parallelogram using coordinate method.
questions.answer: Assign coordinates to vertices ABCD, find midpoints of sides, join them to form quadrilateral; calculate slopes to prove opposite sides parallel.
